title: New Customer Acquisition & CAC
business_definition: New customer acquisition measures how many net-new customers are added per period. CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) = total sales and marketing spend / new customers acquired. Healthy CAC < LTV/3.
diagnostic_questions: Which acquisition channel is driving the most new customers and how is conversion rate by channel?
How does CAC vary by channel, region, or product tier?
What is the payback period — how many months to recover CAC?
Is the quality of acquired customers changing — do newer cohorts retain as well as older ones?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', first_order_date) AS month, acquisition_channel, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS new_customers FROM (SELECT customer_id, MIN(order_date) AS first_order_date, acquisition_channel FROM orders GROUP BY 1, 3) t GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

SELECT channel, SUM(spend) AS total_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT new_customer_id) AS customers, SUM(spend) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT new_customer_id), 0) AS cac FROM marketing_spend LEFT JOIN acquired_customers USING (channel, month) GROUP BY 1

inflation_causes.cause: Reactivated customers counted as new acquisitions
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count, MIN(order_date) AS first_order, MAX(order_date) AS last_order FROM orders GROUP BY 1 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 AND MIN(order_date) != MAX(order_date)

deflation_causes.cause: Customers acquired via non-tracked channels missing from attribution
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT acquisition_channel, COUNT(*) FROM customers GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

anti_patterns: Do not include returning or reactivated customers in new customer count
Do not calculate CAC using only media spend — include salaries and tools in the denominator

sql_assets.new_customers_by_month: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', first_order) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_customers FROM (SELECT customer_id, MIN(order_date) AS first_order FROM orders GROUP BY 1) t GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
